steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: “Though it was designated as mission” is a dependent clause (starts with the subordinating conjunction “though”). After the blank: “mission was actually the ninety-sixth flight...” begins the independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for introductory dependent clauses
steps.content:
The correct answer correctly uses “STS-103, the mission was actually...” placing the required comma after the mission designation.

A period after the mission designation creates a sentence fragment, no punctuation creates a run-on, and inserting “and” awkwardly disrupts the sentence. D is correct.
